sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Newmont Corporation stock faces pressure from 2026 production trough and Nevada dispute amid volatil
feed.summary: Newmont Corporation is facing significant pressure due to a projected production trough in 2026, rising costs, and a dispute with Barrick Gold over their Nevada Gold Mines joint venture. Despite strong Q4 2025 earnings, the company's outlook for 2026, with an anticipated 5.3 million ounces of gold production and higher All-In Sustaining Costs, has dampened investor enthusiasm. The ongoing dispute with Barrick Gold, involving allegations of resource piracy, adds further uncertainty to Newmont's operational stability and future production reliability.

feed.title: Fatburger Opens New Restaurant in Paris Area
feed.summary: FAT Brands Inc. has announced the opening of a new Fatburger restaurant in Rosny-sous-Bois, near Paris, France. This marks the first of 30 planned locations to open across France in partnership with Big M CIE. This expansion is part of Fatburger's strategic growth in the French market, with two more restaurants expected to open later this year.

feed.title: Vonage embeds voice in ServiceNow
feed.summary: Vonage has launched a native integration with ServiceNow Voice, embedding its enterprise-grade voice and real-time AI capabilities directly into ServiceNow's Customer Service Management and IT Service Management platforms. This new solution, which includes multilingual transcription and conversation analysis, eliminates the need for agents to toggle between applications and uses phone calls as triggers for AI-powered automation within ServiceNow. This strategic move enhances Vonage's position beyond communications infrastructure, making it a core part of ServiceNow's workflow and AI layer, while filling a long-standing gap in ServiceNow's customer engagement offerings.

feed.title: Why your credit score might be slipping even if you’re doing everything right
feed.summary: The average American FICO score has dropped to 714, a two-point decline in the last year, primarily due to the end of the student loan payment freeze and an increase in missed mortgage payments. This decline is part of a "K-shaped" economic trend where higher earners maintain or improve their scores, while lower-income individuals and younger generations like Gen Z face greater financial strain from inflation and interest rates. Despite this, the share of Americans with high credit scores (750 and up) has increased, highlighting a growing disparity in financial stability across the population.

feed.title: Equinor ASA (NYSE:EQNR) Raised to "Equal Weight" at Morgan Stanley
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has upgraded Equinor ASA (NYSE:EQNR) from an "underweight" to an "equal weight" rating, setting a price target of $40.40. This upgrade comes despite a consensus "Reduce" recommendation from other brokerages, which maintain an average price target of $34.61. Equinor recently surpassed quarterly earnings estimates and has been involved in positive developments, including a share buyback, a significant lending facility for a joint venture, and new renewable energy projects.

feed.title: FedEx taps Orlando firm OneRail to power same-day shipping as Amazon, others raise stakes
feed.summary: FedEx has partnered with Orlando-based firm OneRail to enhance its same-day delivery services, a move designed to compete with the expedited shipping offerings of major retailers like Amazon, Walmart, and Target. This new service feature was announced on March 24, 2026. The collaboration aims to strengthen FedEx's position in the rapidly evolving logistics market.

feed.title: EQT caps $1.4B debt buyback, proration hits 2027 and 2029 notes
feed.summary: EQT (NYSE: EQT) announced the results of its tender offer to repurchase certain senior notes, with an Aggregate Offer Cap of $1.4 billion. The offer accepted principal amounts for its 3.900% 2027, 6.375% 2029, and 4.50% 2029 notes, with proration applied to the 2027 and one series of 2029 notes. Early settlement for the accepted notes is expected on March 26, 2026, with an early tender premium of $30 per $1,000.

feed.title: Akron-area Westfield Bank branches now First Financial branches
feed.summary: Cincinnati-based First Financial Bank has completed its acquisition of Westfield Bank, converting all Northeast Ohio Westfield Bank branches into First Financial locations. The bank has also allocated $500,000 to its foundation to support local organizations in the communities it now serves. This move follows the purchase of Westfield Bancorp from Ohio Farmers Insurance Company.

feed.title: Evommune (NYSE:EVMN) Stock Price Down 7.6% - Should You Sell?
feed.summary: Shares of Evommune (NYSE:EVMN) fell 7.6% on Tuesday, trading at approximately $21.67 with significantly reduced volume. The clinical-stage biotech company, focused on chronic inflammatory diseases, missed its last earnings target and has a negative P/E ratio. Despite a consensus "Buy" rating from analysts with an average price target of $47.17, one firm, Weiss Ratings, issued a "sell" rating.

feed.title: Arista Networks Inc stock faces pressure amid AI infrastructure slowdown signals in Q1 2026
feed.summary: Arista Networks Inc. (ISIN: US0404131064) shares have declined due to emerging caution in hyperscaler spending, raising concerns about the sustained demand for AI networking infrastructure in Q1 2026. This slowdown signal, particularly from major customers like Microsoft and Meta, has prompted analysts and investors to reassess Arista's near-term growth trajectory despite its strong financial health and AI networking differentiation. The stock's reaction reflects market sensitivity to any deviation from projected revenue expansion, highlighting the fragility of AI-driven growth narratives amid macro uncertainties.

feed.title: Cummins Inc. stock faces pressure amid clean energy shift as Bloom Energy surges 72% in the year
feed.summary: Cummins Inc. is facing pressure due to the global shift towards clean energy, with competitors like Bloom Energy, a fuel cell developer, seeing significant gains (72.28% year-to-date). While Cummins, a leader in diesel engines, is investing in hydrogen and electrification through its Accelera brand, it must accelerate its adaptation to maintain market share and appeal to US investors increasingly focused on low-emission solutions and dividends. The article details Cummins' strategic responses, core strengths, risks, and its path toward carbon neutrality by 2050, highlighting the competitive dynamics between traditional internal combustion and emerging fuel cell technologies.

feed.title: PepsiCo is in the middle of its biggest snack reset in years
feed.summary: PepsiCo is undergoing its most significant snack reset in years, driven by a need to streamline operations, improve margins, and appeal to health-conscious consumers. This involves cutting nearly 20% of its US SKUs and closing plants, while simultaneously pivoting towards functional snacking with added protein and fiber, and expanding into new food territories through acquisitions and new concepts like restaurant-style offerings. The company aims to become a more agile food business, but faces challenges from a competitive market, evolving consumer expectations, and an unstable economic environment.

feed.title: Is Fastenal Company (FAST) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
feed.summary: Fastenal Company (FAST) is highlighted as an attractive investment due to its unique "Onsite model," which creates high customer retention and significant revenue through embedded operations within client facilities. This model provides substantial switching costs and results in strong financial performance, mimicking a "physical SaaS business." Despite not being among typical top hedge fund picks, its recurring revenue dynamics and operational moat make it a compelling investment.

feed.title: Is Elevance Health, Inc. (ELV) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on Elevance Health, Inc. (ELV), while acknowledging near-term profitability pressures. Despite strong revenue growth in 2025, ELV's adjusted EPS declined due to elevated medical costs, particularly affecting Medicaid and Medicare Advantage segments. The company is focusing on operational discipline and growth in its Carelon segment to achieve long-term value creation, though it is not a top pick among hedge funds.

feed.title: Is NVR, Inc. (NVR) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
feed.summary: NVR, Inc. (NVR) is highlighted as a disciplined and cash-generative homebuilder with a strong operational model, featuring an asset-light approach and tiered branding. Despite a recent pullback and high-rate environment, the company maintains robust financials, aggressive share buybacks, and significant free cash flow. While risks like high mortgage rates and construction costs exist, NVR's valuation at 16x earnings and long-term housing undersupply make it attractive for long-term investors.

feed.title: Chart of the Day: Getting Back Into Applied Materials Was a No-Brainer
feed.summary: The article discusses the decision to add Applied Materials (AMAT) back into TheStreet Pro Portfolio, citing its strong stock performance alongside other equipment companies like KLA Tencor and ASML Holding. The author highlights AMAT's consistent higher highs and lows, solid Chaikin money flow, rising stochastics, and new buy signal from MACD, indicating a positive outlook for the stock. Applied Materials is rated as a "buy at anytime" for higher prices.

feed.title: Judge Demands Public Roadmap for Live Nation Settlement as Scrutiny Mounts Over Deal
feed.summary: A federal judge has ordered Live Nation and the Department of Justice to publicly outline their plan for moving the antitrust settlement through Tunney Act review, citing heightened scrutiny over the deal. The settlement, which allowed Live Nation to avoid a breakup, is controversial due to allegations of political influence from former President Donald Trump and criticisms that it doesn't adequately address Live Nation's market power. This order signals that the settlement will undergo thorough examination, with the judge emphasizing transparency and public interest.

feed.title: 3 Reasons We Love First Solar (FSLR)
feed.summary: Despite a recent stock price decline, First Solar (FSLR) shows strong fundamentals making it a potentially attractive investment. The company has demonstrated impressive growth with a 14% compounded annual growth rate in sales over five years, expanding free cash flow margins, and a significant increase in Return on Invested Capital (ROIC). These factors highlight First Solar's robust business model and expanding competitive advantage in the solar energy market.

feed.title: Linde plc stock faces helium demand surge from semiconductors amid industrial gas market shifts
feed.summary: Linde plc is experiencing increased demand for electronic-grade helium due to the expanding semiconductor industry, particularly driven by AI and data center development. The company's strategic position in supplying critical gases for chip manufacturing, alongside its involvement in clean energy projects like blue and green hydrogen production, positions it for sustained growth. US investors are eyeing Linde for its exposure to these high-tech supply chains and energy transition initiatives, despite potential risks from energy cost volatility and competition.

feed.title: KLA Corporation stock surges on $7 billion buyback announcement and 21% dividend hike amid AI chip boom
feed.summary: KLA Corporation announced a significant $7 billion share repurchase program and a 21% increase in its quarterly dividend, signaling strong confidence in its leadership in semiconductor process control. This move comes at a crucial time as advanced packaging technologies become essential for next-generation AI chips, fueling demand for KLA's inspection and metrology tools. The capital return strategy underscores the company's robust cash generation and positive outlook on long-term demand from hyperscalers and foundries investing in AI infrastructure.

feed.title: [144] Keysight Technologies, Inc. SEC Filing
feed.summary: This article details a Form 144 SEC filing by Keysight Technologies, Inc. (KEYS). The filing indicates a proposed sale of 2,000 shares of common stock, valued at $594,380.20, acquired through restricted stock vesting as compensation. The sale is scheduled for March 24, 2026, on the NYSE.

feed.title: Potomac Edison Maryland Customers Can Get Help with Seasonal Energy Costs
feed.summary: Potomac Edison reminds Maryland customers about various bill assistance programs available to help manage rising energy costs due to seasonal temperature changes. These programs, including MEAP, EUSP, and USPP, offer grants, financial aid, and protection from service disruptions. The company encourages eligible households to explore these resources to ensure peace of mind regarding their energy bills.

feed.title: Jack Henry & Associates opens entries for 2026 Cobalt Awards recognizing industry innovation
feed.summary: Jack Henry & Associates has opened submissions for its 2026 Cobalt Awards, inviting innovators to showcase impactful solutions in financial technology. Entrants have until April 30 to submit their ideas for this prestigious industry recognition. This initiative continues Jack Henry's efforts to foster innovation, building on previous forums and webinars focused on digital banking, cybersecurity, and platform utilization for credit unions.

feed.title: Hershey Company Stock Rises 2.4% on NYSE Amid Strong Analyst Upgrades and Institutional Buying
feed.summary: The Hershey Company's stock rose 2.4% on the NYSE to $215.23, driven by increased institutional investment and positive analyst ratings. This surge follows strong Q4 earnings, raised FY2026 guidance, and a dividend hike, reinforcing Hershey's position as a stable consumer staples stock for US investors amidst market volatility. Analysts have upgraded their price targets, and the company's low beta makes it an attractive defensive play.

feed.title: Revvity Inc. stock faces pressure amid life sciences sector slowdown and acquisition integration cha
feed.summary: Revvity Inc. is experiencing pressure due to softening demand in the diagnostics and research markets, as well as challenges integrating recent acquisitions like BioLegend. Despite reaffirming full-year 2026 guidance, investors are skeptical given a 2.1% stock dip post-earnings and the broader sector's struggles post-pandemic. The company's Enterprise Services segment, focusing on newborn screening, provides some stability, while its stock trades at a discount, offering potential for patient investors betting on a life sciences recovery.

feed.title: Caesars Entertainment Breaks New Ground in Online Gaming
feed.summary: Caesars Entertainment has become the first online casino operator to offer Aristocrat Interactive slot titles in West Virginia, bringing popular land-based casino games to online platforms like Caesars Palace Online Casino and Caesars Sportsbook & Casino. This initiative expands Caesars' online gaming offerings, including future releases and exclusive promotions through its Caesars Rewards program, enhancing the player experience and attracting a wider audience. The launch is part of Caesars' broader strategy to provide diverse, top-tier gaming options across all approved jurisdictions.

feed.title: Eni reports 3.3 billion dollar credit exposure to Venezuela at end-2025
feed.summary: Italian energy company Eni reported a credit exposure of $3.3 billion to Venezuela's state-owned oil company, Pdvsa, as of the end of 2025. Approximately $1 billion of this amount represents accrued interest. This information was disclosed in Eni's 2025 financial statements.

feed.title: No evidence Valero Texas refinery blast caused by intentional act, sheriff’s office By Reuters
feed.summary: An explosion at Valero Energy Corp's Port Arthur, Texas refinery on Monday night was not caused by an intentional act, according to the Jefferson County Sheriff's office. The blast occurred in a diesel hydrotreater and was felt 11 miles away, but no injuries were reported as Valero shut down the refinery to contain the fire.

feed.title: AMD Rides on Accelerating Data Center Growth: A Sign of More Upside?
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is experiencing rapid growth in its Data Center revenues, driven by strong demand for its EPYC processors and Instinct MI350 series GPUs, with significant adoption by hyperscalers and increased on-prem deployments. While the company's data center AI business is accelerating, AMD faces tough competition from NVIDIA and Broadcom. Despite outperforming its sector year-to-date, AMD's stock is currently considered overvalued based on its price/sales ratio.

feed.title: Smart Waste Management Market to Reach USD 7.8 Billion by 2033, Driven by 16.2% CAGR
feed.summary: The smart waste management market is projected to grow from USD 2.7 billion in 2026 to USD 7.8 billion by 2033, exhibiting a CAGR of 16.2%. This growth is fueled by the integration of IoT, data analytics, and automation in waste management, driven by urbanization, sustainability mandates, and regulatory frameworks. North America and Europe are current leaders, with Asia-Pacific showing strong growth potential in this evolving market.

feed.title: Brooks Tractor Inc. Becomes Trimble Technology Outlet Serving Customers in South, Eastern Wisconsin
feed.summary: Brooks Tractor Inc. has become the newest Trimble Technology Outlet, providing Trimble's grade control, site positioning systems, and correction services directly to customers using John Deere earthmoving equipment in South and Eastern Wisconsin. This partnership aims to improve jobsite productivity and profitability by offering streamlined workflows and enhanced efficiency. Brooks Tractor will provide installation and training, reinforcing its 80-year tradition of excellence and dedication to offering advanced technology solutions.

feed.title: Keysight Technologies (KEYS) director granted 870 shares in equity award
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies director Michelle Johnston Holthaus received an equity award of 870 shares of common stock, granted as immediately vested restricted stock units under the company's 2014 plan. This transaction increased her direct holdings to 8,483 shares. The Form 4 filing indicates this was a stock award, not a market purchase, aligning her interests with shareholders.

feed.title: DaVita Inc. stock faces scrutiny amid dialysis sector reimbursement pressures and Medicare policy sh
feed.summary: DaVita Inc., a leading U.S. dialysis provider, faces scrutiny over reimbursement pressures from Medicare Advantage plans and evolving federal policies. Despite these challenges, the company demonstrates operational strength through its extensive network and a pivot towards higher-margin home dialysis. Investors are watching Q1 earnings for indications of patient retention and operational efficiency, considering the stock a defensive play in a volatile market due to its stable financial health and cash-generative model.

feed.title: Tesla files site plan for 'ecological paradise' beside Austin gigafactory
feed.summary: Tesla has filed a site plan for a park touted as an "ecological paradise" adjacent to its Austin gigafactory, an idea Elon Musk first proposed five years ago. Locals are expected to have access to the riverfront park. The announcement comes amidst public skepticism surrounding the project.

feed.title: KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Price Target Lowered to $106.00 at BMO Capital Markets
feed.summary: BMO Capital Markets has lowered its price target for KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) to $106.00 from $125.00, while maintaining an "outperform" rating. Several other analysts have also adjusted their price targets for KKR, though the consensus remains a "Moderate Buy" with an average target of $149.00. The article also highlights recent significant insider buying, with CEO Joseph Y. Bae and Director Matt Cohler purchasing a substantial number of shares.

feed.title: A United economy row turns into a couch on long-haul flights
feed.summary: United Airlines is introducing the "United Relax Row," a three-seat Economy row that converts into a couch-like, mattress-style space for long-haul flights. This new seating option is expected to launch in 2027 and will be deployed on over 200 Boeing 787 and 777 widebody aircraft by 2030, with United holding North American exclusivity on the design. The Relax Row aims to provide enhanced comfort for families, couples, and solo travelers, complete with amenities like mattress pads, blankets, and plush kits for children.

feed.title: Pentair plc stock faces pressure amid industrial slowdown signals and rising input costs
feed.summary: Pentair plc's stock is experiencing pressure due to mixed signals in water treatment demand in key US markets and broader industrial headwinds. The company's residential pool segment is softening, while its industrial segment remains stable, though rising input costs pose a challenge to margins. Despite a solid balance sheet and a focus on essential water technologies, risks such as prolonged consumer weakness and commodity volatility require close monitoring by investors.

feed.title: Erste Group initiates RTX stock with buy on defense demand
feed.summary: Erste Group initiated coverage on RTX Corp. (NYSE:RTX) with a buy rating, citing strong demand for engines and increasing global defense spending. Despite InvestingPro's indication that RTX is currently overvalued, the firm expects the share price to continue rising due to the company's growth trajectory and valuation metrics. RTX has shown strong performance, expanding production, and projecting significant revenue and EPS growth for fiscal year 2026.

feed.title: July 15 payout: SoCalGas declares two $0.375 preferred dividends
feed.summary: Southern California Gas Company (SoCalGas) announced regular quarterly preferred dividends of $0.375 per share for both its Preferred Stock and Preferred Stock, Series A. These dividends are payable on July 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of June 10, 2026. This declaration aligns with the company's regular quarterly payout schedule for preferred shares.

feed.title: Research Alert: CFRA Lowers Rating On Shares Of Resmed Inc. To Hold From Buy
feed.summary: CFRA has lowered its rating on ResMed Inc. shares from Buy to Hold. This change reflects CFRA's updated investment perspective on the company.

feed.title: Equinor (EQNR) Initiates Drilling at Brazil's $9B Raia Field
feed.summary: Equinor (EQNR) has begun development drilling at the Raia pre-salt field in Brazil's Campos Basin, representing its largest international investment at $9 billion. The field is estimated to hold over 1 billion barrels of oil equivalent and aims to start production in 2028, potentially meeting 15% of Brazil's natural gas demand. Despite a decline in three-year revenue growth, Equinor maintains strong operational efficiency and adequate liquidity, though its Altman Z-Score suggests potential financial stress.

feed.title: PulteGroup Inc. stock faces headwinds from rising mortgage rates and slowing homebuyer demand in 2026 housing market
feed.summary: PulteGroup Inc., a leading US homebuilder, is navigating a challenging 2026 housing market due to persistent high mortgage rates and slowing demand. Despite resilient gross margins and strong cash flow, the company faces declining closings and a shrinking order backlog. PulteGroup is strategically managing land investments and cost controls, with long-term demand tailwinds offering potential upside for investors once interest rates stabilize.

feed.title: Chevron to Buy Oil from Sable Offshore for Californians
feed.summary: Chevron Corp. announced a deal to purchase up to 20,000 barrels per day of crude oil from Sable Offshore Corp.'s Santa Ynez Unit off Santa Barbara. This move, stemming from federal intervention using the Defense Production Act, aims to boost California's energy security and significantly reduce its reliance on foreign oil imports. The partnership is expected to create jobs and economic benefits within California, providing a temporary relief amidst the state's challenging energy policy landscape.

feed.title: Packaging Corp of America stock faces steady demand amid packaging sector resilience in 2026
feed.summary: Packaging Corp of America (ISIN: US6951561022) is projected to experience steady demand in 2026 due to the resilience of the packaging sector, particularly benefiting from e-commerce growth and operational efficiency. The company, trading on the NYSE, maintains strong financial performance with high utilization rates and cost controls, appealing to US investors seeking stable returns and sustainability-focused investments. Despite potential headwinds like raw material price volatility, its integrated model and domestic focus position it as a defensive pick.

feed.title: This Week in News: Live Nation Deal, Texas HUB Lawsuit, Jan. 6 Bill And Arizona Probe | Essence
feed.summary: This week's news highlights significant decisions from lawmakers and government leaders. A tentative agreement has been reached between the Justice Department and Live Nation regarding their 2024 lawsuit, while Texas business owners are challenging the dismantling of the state’s 35-year-old HUB program. Virginia lawmakers passed a bill establishing guidelines on how K-12 classrooms should teach about the Jan. 6 events, and Arizona is facing a Trump administration investigation into its 2020 election results.

feed.title: McCormick (MKC) VP Schwartz updates holdings after RSU vesting and tax withholding
feed.summary: McCormick & Co. Vice President and General Counsel Jeffery D. Schwartz reported an update to his holdings following the vesting of Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) and subsequent tax withholdings. On February 15, 2026, Schwartz acquired 4,126 shares through RSU exercise and had 7,616 shares withheld for tax obligations at $71.61 per share. An amended Form 4/A was filed to correct a previous administrative error in the reported beneficial ownership amount, showing Schwartz now directly holds 87,818 shares of McCormick & Co. voting common stock.

feed.title: MGM Resorts International | 3/24/26
feed.summary: Mari Gatti, VP of Philanthropy at MGM Resorts International, shared her leadership journey and how her passion for philanthropy drives community impact. She discussed MGM Resorts' approach to giving back through partnerships, contributions, and employee volunteerism, emphasizing leadership as a means to uplift others and make a lasting difference. The segment highlights how purpose and passion guide her success in building stronger communities.

feed.title: Truist Financial Lowers Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE:NCLH) Price Target to $25.00
feed.summary: Truist Financial has lowered its price target for Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE:NCLH) from $26.00 to $25.00, while maintaining a "buy" rating, indicating a potential upside of 26.6% from its previous close. Despite beating EPS estimates slightly ($0.28 vs. $0.27), the company missed revenue expectations. Analyst sentiment remains a "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $25.40, though several firms have adjusted their forecasts post-earnings.

feed.title: Caesars Entertainment Breaks New Ground in Online Gaming
feed.summary: Caesars Entertainment has become the first online casino operator to offer Aristocrat Interactive slot titles in West Virginia, including popular games like 5 Dragons™ and Fu Dai Lian Lian™ Panda. This expansion allows players to access beloved physical casino games on their electronic devices, enhancing Caesars' online gaming portfolio across various approved jurisdictions. The company plans further expansion with additional Aristocrat titles and exclusive promotions through its Caesars Rewards® program.

feed.title: Truist Financial Boosts Royal Caribbean Cruises (NYSE:RCL) Price Target to $327.00
feed.summary: Truist Financial has raised its price target for Royal Caribbean Cruises (NYSE:RCL) to $327.00 from $318.00, maintaining a "hold" rating, while other firms have also increased targets, leading to a "Moderate Buy" consensus and target of $348.95. Despite a new $2.0 billion share buyback authorization, the stock traded down 2.4% at $272.16, with significant insider selling possibly creating mixed signals for investors.
